request.question: Introducing Sporomusa ovata into a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE will fix CO₂ into acetate at a rate of at least 150 mmol/L/day, outperforming current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks by at least 20%.

plan.objective: To determine the rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate by Sporomusa ovata in a bioelectrochemical system at a cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE.
plan.experimental_design: This experiment will compare the CO₂ fixation rates of Sporomusa ovata introduced into a bioelectrochemical system against current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmarks. The key gating logic involves measuring acetate production over a defined period at the specified cathode potential.
controls: Current biocatalytic carbon capture benchmark using Acetobacterium woodii.
Blank bioelectrochemical system without any microbial inoculation.

step_by_step_protocol.description: Prepare the bioelectrochemical system by assembling the electrochemical cell, ensuring all components are sterile. Prepare the growth medium for Sporomusa ovata, including necessary nutrients and buffers. Ensure the cathode is set to a potential of −400mV vs SHE. Safety note: Handle all reagents according to safety data sheets. Expected duration: 2 hours.

Inoculate the bioelectrochemical system with Sporomusa ovata at a concentration of 10^6 cells/mL. Start the electrochemical reaction by applying the −400mV potential. Monitor the system for 24 hours. Safety note: Ensure proper ventilation and use personal protective equipment (PPE) when handling cultures. Expected duration: 24h incubation.

After 24 hours, collect samples from the bioelectrochemical system for acetate quantification. Use high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC) to measure acetate concentration. Prepare a standard curve using known acetate concentrations for accurate quantification. Safety note: Handle HPLC reagents with care and follow lab safety protocols. Expected duration: 3 hours.

Simultaneously, run the positive control using Acetobacterium woodii under the same conditions to compare CO₂ fixation rates. Collect samples after 24 hours and analyze using the same HPLC method. Expected duration: 3 hours.

Analyze the data collected from HPLC for both Sporomusa ovata and the positive control. Calculate the rate of CO₂ fixation into acetate for both conditions. Safety note: Ensure proper disposal of waste according to hazardous waste guidelines. Expected duration: 2 hours.

assumptions: Sporomusa ovata will grow and fix CO₂ at the specified cathode potential of −400mV vs SHE.
The HPLC method will accurately quantify acetate concentrations in the samples.
The bioelectrochemical system is functioning properly and is free from contamination.
decision_criteria: If the rate of acetate production from Sporomusa ovata is greater than 150 mmol/L/day, consider it a successful outcome.
If the acetate production is less than the positive control, further optimization may be needed.
If there are significant deviations in expected results, investigate potential sources of error.
reproducibility_notes: Record the lot numbers of all reagents used in the experiment.
Document the specific conditions of the bioelectrochemical system setup, including temperature and pH.
Maintain a detailed log of all measurements and observations during the experiment.

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 150 mmol/L/day improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.
